seoTitle: How to Send an Email With Embedded Image in Power Automate
metaDescription: Learn how to build HTML emails in Power Automate with images embedded directly in the body using CID or image URLs.
intro: Sending professional emails with images shown inline, rather than as attachments, makes automated notifications look far more polished. This guide covers how to build an HTML email in Power Automate with embedded images using the Outlook connector.

steps.heading: Create a new cloud flow
steps.body: Start a new flow in Power Automate with a trigger that fits your use case, such as a scheduled or event-based trigger.

steps.heading: Build the HTML email template
steps.body: Write the HTML content for your email body, structuring it so image placeholders can be embedded within the text.

steps.heading: Embed images in the email body
steps.body: Use Content-ID (CID) references or publicly accessible image URLs so the images render inline rather than as separate attachments.

steps.heading: Configure Outlook and test the flow
steps.body: Set up the Outlook send email action with your HTML content, then run the flow and check that images display correctly across email clients.

faq.question: What's the difference between using CID and an image URL?
faq.answer: CID embeds the image file directly with the email, while an image URL pulls the image from a public web location each time the email is opened.

faq.question: Will embedded images work in every email client?
faq.answer: Compatibility can vary between email clients, so it's worth testing your email in a few common clients before relying on it broadly.

faq.question: Can I use this for automated newsletters or reports?
faq.answer: Yes, embedded image emails work well for branded newsletters, automated reports, and approval notifications.

faq.question: Do I need coding skills to build the HTML email?
faq.answer: Basic HTML knowledge helps for structuring the email body, though many teams start from a template and adjust it as needed.
